name: sqlite-utils (SQLite Toolkit)

auth.notes: Local SQLite file access; no auth

performance.notes: SQLite native speed; bulk insert with executemany; WAL mode for concurrent reads

agent_readiness.notes: Python library and CLI for SQLite data manipulation. Schema-on-write: insert dicts and tables are auto-created. Alter columns automatically. Upsert with unique key. Full-text search (FTS5). Embeddings column support. Bulk insert from CSV/JSON/ndjson. Transform and extract operations. CLI for quick data import: `sqlite-utils insert data.db table data.csv --csv`. Works with Datasette for publishing. Excellent for ETL pipelines and data wrangling into SQLite.

gotchas: Schema-on-write can create messy schemas if input data is inconsistent
Not suitable for high-concurrency multi-writer scenarios
Type inference can be incorrect for edge cases
best_when: ETL pipelines loading data into SQLite
Quick data wrangling and exploration via CLI
LLM tool use requiring persistent structured storage
summary: sqlite-utils is Python's friendliest SQLite toolkit — schema-on-write (insert dicts, tables auto-created), upsert, FTS5 full-text search, CSV/JSON/ndjson bulk import, and a powerful CLI. Simon Willison's companion to Datasette. Excellent for ETL pipelines, LLM tool persistence, data wrangling, and rapid prototyping with SQLite. High agent-friendliness due to ergonomic Python API and predictable behavior.
